item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image is owned and copyrighted by Morquip (as indicated here), and the second image could not be found under any source as cc-compliant. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit by StevenThePotato was reverted, as it added an additional word that changed the meaning of a sentence, which is considered a stylistic edit and as such requires permission from the original author or staff to be made.

Editors, if you're not fixing (non-intentionally mistaken) grammar, mechanics, or formatting, please obtain permission prior to making changes to a page. This is noted in the Site Rules.

item.title: A little late, I know.
item.content:encoded: 3/11/2016 09:51:02 GMT — All personnel at Site-35 are instructed to stay away from Maximum Security Morgue 3-A until containment specialists have finished constructing the barrier. H. Kazmir | Director, Site-35

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept itself presents a good idea for an anomaly, but it feels much more like an anomalous object than an item deserving an SCP classification. Over 40 years without a notable bit of dialogue from the corpse, and no full reanimation, why keep it around?

If this had a more compelling reason to warrant study (hints in the recovery log or corpse dialogue perhaps), I'd feel more compelled to to upvote the article, as it is quite well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me the ambiguity is what sells it. It could either be "some great horror struck this village, left this guy how he is and then no one spoke of it beyond their great terror"… or it just happened and people panicked as they would in real life at a such an occurence and merely considered "screw that place, it's totally cursed".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i want to like this, but it leaves me wanting more info in a bad way :(

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: Self-upvoting with staff permission to counteract confirmed case of malicious downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my sides

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Has the Foundation not attempted to take any tissue samples? Are are any samples taken drawn back into the corpse?

Despite the presence of fresh-water springs and strategic positioning atop a ridge, combatants from both sides of the ongoing conflict avoided all contact with the village site,

Interesting, but I think the Foundation would try to do a little followup on that, interview some of the surviving combatants.

You owe your old father that (indecipherable) I still see him sometimes. Deep in the jungle.

Is this meant to imply that that his father was either a walking undead, or a corpse in the same condition as the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel sad for the poor corpse (even if it is just a "jukebox" with no self-awareness, as implied by lack of brain activity)

The "happiest day of my life" thing, is especially touching.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed "esophagus" to "trachea", as they're different tubes and the esophagus has nothing to do with making noises (unless you count prevention of the "I'm choking" noise :P).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As said by others, the utterances and overall concept isn't all that compelling. Perhaps if some more clues or details were given regarding what happened in the village and why the soldiers avoided it may help make it more interesting than just a poor guy who can't die. Definite potential but no vote for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda interesting, but it didn't really amount to much by the end. There's no narrative. I still upvoted, because the effect of what the corpse is going through is pretty interesting, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, it's pretty much a corpse what won't stay dead and says mysterious things. Not doing anything for me.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simply doesn't blow my skirt up. Not sure if I'm in favor of keeping it around or not.

Abstaining for now. Maybe in a few weeks I'll get back 'round to it and decide how I feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has no talking non-human animals in it, so the only logical conclusion I can reach is that you're actually a furry version of me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That mental image improved an already-good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like the kind of article I would have written.

Kalinin, are you me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this had a very creepy atmosphere to it, especially in the paragraph explaining how it was recovered.

following Foundation observation of unusual troop movements during a 1970 North Vietnamese Army offensive. Despite the presence of fresh-water springs and strategic positioning atop a ridge, combatants from both sides of the ongoing conflict avoided all contact with the village site

Structures in the immediate vicinity of the hut had been burned approximately two weeks prior to the arrival of the investigative team.

That kind of stuff creeps me out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty neat. Loved the notes on the end, though I'm struggling to find if there's any significance to the dates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad at all, but I'm not particularly drawn in by it. Also, fun fact, Leej is a nickname I have for one of my friends. I was kind of surprised to see it being used in an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has a creepy feel to it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The vocalizations made it better for me. When a corpse talks in an article it's usually: "Doom this! Doom that! Beware the tentacled horror that eats souls and poops demons!" This one just provides us with chitchat.

I can even see the researchers getting frustrated because they're waiting for some grand proclamation of an upcoming end-of-the-world scenario from this thing and all it wants to do is rehash last night's cockfight and drinking binge.

"Goddammit, he's talking about the price of sheep. AGAIN."
"I'm sorry, sir. Still nothing on possible XK-class scenarios."
"Well, keep monitoring that bastard. He's bound to lapse into doomsayer-mode soon."

That idea made me chuckle, so +1.

Edit: Also, the idea that we might have Maximum Security Morgues is horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a corpse that doesn't properly decompose, most likely because it hasn't been buried the way it was supposed to. Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of a guy continually decomposing randomly and re-forming is pretty cool, but all of the stuff about the vocalizations doesn't do anything for me. It brings the rest of the article down, IMO.
